mnemonics: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (OSI layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
TCP: Talk Carefully Please (reliable), UDP: Unreliable Datagram Protocol (fast)
A Alligators (1-126), B Bears (128-191), C Cats (192-223) for IP classes
Borrow Bits, Build Subnets for subnet calculation
Distance Vector: Rumors Spread (e.g., RIP), Link State: Full Map Known (e.g., OSPF)
Hub: Everyone Hears, Switch: Knows Where to Go, Router: Directs Traffic
Get Post Put Delete for HTTP methods
Check Home First, Then Ask Outside for DNS lookup
IPv4: 4 Groups decimal, IPv6: 8 Groups HeX
keyPoints: TCP vs UDP: reliability vs speed
Routing protocols: Distance Vector and Link State
Network devices: hub, switch, router roles
HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
commonMistakes: Confusing IP class ranges (e.g., Class A starts at 1)
Mixing TCP (reliable) and UDP (unreliable) use cases
Using RIP for large networks (not scalable)
